---
title: "Capstone — Reproducible Spatial Pipeline"
toc: true
---

The capstone ties the whole module together: take a district-level health indicator from raw
data all the way to a clustered, mapped, interpreted result — as a **reproducible pipeline**,
not a one-off script.

## What you build

A full pipeline on district-level health data (the bundled AHS/NFHS example, or an indicator
of your choice):

1. **Profile** the raw data — `capstone/R/02-profile-document.R`
2. **Clean & validate** — `capstone/R/03-clean-validate.R`
3. **Tidy & reshape** — `capstone/R/04-tidy-reshape.R`
4. **Transform** (derive indicators) — `capstone/R/05-transform.R`
5. **EDA & plot** — `capstone/R/06-eda-plot.R`
6. **Analyse** (model + spatial clustering) — `capstone/R/07-analyse.R`

Run the whole chain with **`capstone/run-all.R`**, which produces the figures and tables that
`capstone/report.qmd` knits into a final report.

For the clustering step on its own (Queen contiguity → spatial weights → Moran's I → LISA),
see **`capstone/ahs_clustering_exercise.r`**.

## Deliverable

Pick a public-health indicator (stunting, wasting, underweight, anaemia, IMR, …) and submit:

- the reproducible script(s) / Quarto document,
- a global + local clustering analysis (Moran's I + LISA cluster map),
- a **500-word interpretation** aimed at a public-health decision-maker.

::: {.callout-note}
## Running the capstone
The capstone scripts generate their own `outputs/` (data, figures) when you run
`capstone/run-all.R`. Run it once before knitting `capstone/report.qmd`.
:::
